page: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Operating on the arm64 architecture, this instance provides 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. It features 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. No local storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data highlights the instance's strong memory subsystem. It achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth tests, recording 402.49 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 183.47 GB/sec for large-block write operations. PassMark database operations and memory scores also rank in the top tier. Multi-core processing is robust, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 21614.0 placing in the top 10%. Single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, show strong performance, while multi-threaded decompression and standard CPU stress tests perform in the average range.

The instance exhibits certain performance tradeoffs, particularly in LLM inference workloads. While prompt processing for medium-sized 7B models is average, text generation for small 135M models and prompt processing for large 70B models fall into the lower performance tiers. Given the high memory density and top-tier database operation benchmarks, the r8g.8xlarge is highly optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases like Redis, relational database operations, and large-scale data processing.
description: The Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated arm64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, but contains no local storage or GPUs. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier memory performance, with large-block read bandwidth reaching 402.49 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 183.47 GB/sec, alongside strong database operation scores. However, it exhibits weaker performance in specific LLM inference tasks. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, and in-memory caching platforms like Redis.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance delivering high-bandwidth memory performance and dedicated core processing for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated arm64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 at 2.8 GHz.
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.
Top-tier memory read and write bandwidth exceeding 400 GB/sec and 180 GB/sec respectively.
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Optimized for memory-bound workloads, relational databases, and Redis caching.
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.
